data.title: New bid for 7-Eleven owner might derail Couche-Tard and include U.S. IPO
data.description:
Another hat may soon be thrown in the Slurpee chiller. The Ito family of Japan, the founders of the 7-Eleven chain in the country, is set to propose a ¥9T ($60B) buyout of Seven & i Holdings (OTCPK:SVNDF), according to sources at Bloomberg. The bid would go up against a ¥7.1T ($47B) offer by Canada's Alimentation Couche-Tard (OTCPK:ANCTF), a multinational convenience store operator that owns rival Circle K in the U.S.

Backdrop: Long under pressure from activist shareholders, Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDF) quickly rejected an initial bid from Couche-Tard (OTCPK:ANCTF) in September, saying it undervalued its business and didn't take regulatory concerns into account. A higher offer was eventually made, but fresh efforts are in the works to keep the brand from falling into hands overseas. A bid by the founding Ito family would stave off the largest takeover in Japan and result in Seven & i (OTCPK:SVNDF) splitting into three distinct entities.

The first would raise more than ¥1T in cash ($6.6B) via the IPO of North American 7-Eleven stores (as well as its Speedway and Sunoco gas station subsidiaries). Those establishments generated $70.3B in revenue during the most recent fiscal year, showing the power and reach of the business. Another planned unit would include 7-Eleven convenience stores in Japan, while the last division was already announced in October and would be created by spinning off Seven & i's (OTCPK:SVNDF) non-core Japanese businesses like supermarkets, specialty stores, and retail outlets.

Some history: Starting out in 1927 as a convenience store attached to Southland Ice Company shops (this was before mainstream refrigeration), the locations eventually gained in popularity by staying open on weekends and for notable branding efforts that made them known as Tote'm Stores. The name was changed to 7-Eleven in 1946 to reflect expanded business hours, while U.S. franchising began in 1961, and in 1973, Ito-Yokado signed an agreement to develop 7-Eleven convenience stores in Japan. In 1991, Ito-Yokado acquired a 70% stake in Southland Corporation, which the conglomerate later expanded to 100% in 2005, when its collective businesses were reorganized into the holding company known as Seven & i Holdings (OTCPK:SVNDF).

More on 7-Eleven

  • Seven & i: Undervalued With Good Overseas Growth Potential
  • FTC warns Couche-Tard, 7-Eleven parent to retain documents on potential deal
  • Global companies with at least 50% U.S. revenue exposure as Trump eyes tariffs: BofA
  • Alimentation Couche-Tard Bid Places Seven & i's Investment Merits In The Spotlight
  • Japan's 7-Eleven considers going private to fend off foreign takeover - reports

data.title: Chinese industry groups say US chips are 'no longer safe' - report
data.description:
Four of China's top industry associations said that Chinese companies should be careful of buying U.S. chips as they are "no longer safe" and purchase locally, Reuters reported.

Earlier this week, the U.S. Department of Commerce placed additional curbs on China's access to vital tools and components for AI chips. The two countries have been in a tech tussle for years. Recently, U.S. President-elect Donald Trump promised to impose heavy tariffs on imported goods from China.

The industry association warnings come on the heels of the new U.S. curbs, which also saw the department blacklisting an additional 140 Chinese entities that it said have been operating on behalf of the Chinese government.

Their advice could affect U.S. companies such as Nvidia (NVDA), AMD (AMD) and Intel (INTC) which, despite export controls, have managed to sell products in China, the report added.

The Internet Society of China asked local companies to think carefully before buying U.S. chips and seek to boost cooperation with chip companies from countries and regions other than the U.S., the report noted citing the trade body's official WeChat account.

The association also encouraged local companies to "proactively" use chips made by both domestic and foreign-owned companies in China.

U.S. chip export controls have led to "substantial harm" to the health and development of China's internet industry, the trade body noted. Companies targeted by the U.S. said they would be able to continue manufacturing due to their efforts to localize output, according to the report.

The China Association of Communication Enterprises noted that it no longer saw U.S. chip products as reliable or safe and the Chinese government should probe how secure the supply chain of the country's critical information infrastructure was, the report added.

The statements did not show why U.S. chips were unsafe or unreliable, as per the report.

The warnings remind of China's treatment of Micron (MU), which saw a cybersecurity review last year after the U.S. imposed export controls on chip making technology to China. In October, the Cybersecurity Association of China asked that Intel's products sold in China be subject to a security review, alleging the company had "constantly harmed" China's national security and interests. Intel had responded, saying that it would look forward to working with the relevant officials on the matter to clarify any questions that may exist.

The Semiconductor Industry Association, a U.S. trade body representing major chipmakers, said, "Coordinated calls in China to limit procurement of U.S. chips are unhelpful, and any claims that American chips are 'no longer safe or reliable' are simply inaccurate," the report noted.

The association reaffirmed its belief that export controls should be narrow and targeted to meet specific national security objectives, and requested both governments to avoid further escalation.

On Tuesday, China also banned exports of rare minerals used in military applications, solar cells, fibre optic cables and other production processes. A White House National Security Council spokesperson said the U.S. would take the required steps to try to deter other "coercive actions" from China and continue efforts to diversify supply chains away from the country, the report added.

The U.S., China, the EU, South Korea and Japan are among countries and regions which have stepped up efforts to boost domestic chip manufacturing to stay ahead in the AI race.

The U.S. and its allies including the Netherlands, Germany, South Korea and Japan have been tightening curbs on China's access to advanced semiconductor technology.

data.title: Himax Technologies board authorizes $20M share repurchase program
data.description:
• Himax Technologies (NASDAQ:HIMX) said on Wednesday that its board has approved an up to $20M repurchase program of the company’s American Depository Shares (ADS), effective immediately.
  • "This share buyback program underscores our commitment to enhancing shareholder value and confidence in Himax’s market leadership and long-term growth prospects, particularly in automotive, AI, WLO, and OLED," said Jordan Wu, President and Chief Executive Officer of Himax.
  • The share repurchase amount of $20 million represents ~2.10% of the market cap of $953.99 million as of Dec 03, 2024.

data.title: Biggest stock movers Wednesday: CRM, MRVL, and more
data.description:
Stock futures edged higher during premarket hours on Wednesday following the release of the October JOLTS report, which showed job openings rising to 7.744M, surpassing the 7.490M consensus.

Here are some of Wednesday's biggest stock movers:

Biggest stock gainers

  • Salesforce (CRM) shares soared nearly 11% despite mixed FQ3 results, driven by consistent revenue and subscription growth of 8% and 9% YoY, respectively. For FQ4, Salesforce guided revenue of $9.9B–$10.1B (vs. $10.5B consensus) and EPS of $2.57–$2.62 (vs. $2.65 consensus). For FY2025, the company expects revenue of $37.8B–$38B (midpoint: $37.9B, slightly above $37.86B consensus) and EPS of $9.98–$10.03 (midpoint: $10.01, below $10.11 consensus).
  • Shares of Marvell Technology (MRVL) jumped 11% after reporting strong FQ3 results and an upbeat FQ4 outlook, driven by artificial intelligence. Data center revenue surged 98% Y/Y. For Q4, Marvell expects revenue of $1.8B (+/- 5%) vs. $1.65B consensus, with gross margins of ~50% and adjusted margins of ~60%. It expects adjusted EPS of $0.59 (+/- $0.05) vs. the consensus of $0.52 for Q4. CEO Matt Murphy highlighted 19% sequential revenue growth for Q4 and projected FY2026 momentum, calling this 'the beginning of a new era of growth for Marvell.'
  • Okta (OKTA) shares surged 15% after delivering strong fiscal Q3 results and raising its FY2025 guidance. For Q4, the company projects adjusted EPS of $0.73–$0.74 (vs. $0.67 consensus) and revenue of $667M–$669M (vs. $650.7M consensus). For FY2025, Okta raised its adjusted EPS forecast to $2.75–$2.76 (up from $2.58–$2.63 and vs. $2.61 consensus) and revenue to $2.595B–$2.597B (up from $2.56B–$2.57B), representing 15% YoY growth.

Biggest stock losers

  • Gaotu Techedu (GOTU) shares plunged over 9% following FQ3 results as escalating losses overshadowed a 53% Y/Y increase in revenue and a 67.2% rise in gross billings to RMB1,069.2M. The company reported a sharp rise in net loss to RMB471.3M compared to RMB57.7M in 3Q23, with gross profit margin falling to 64.4% from 72.4% a year ago. Operating expenses surged 89.1% Y/Y, driven by increased marketing efforts and workforce expansion.

data.title: Rio Tinto targets higher copper production in 2025 driven by Mongolia's Oyu Tolgoi
data.description:
Rio Tinto (NYSE:RIO) on Wednesday forecast higher copper output in the coming year as the miner ramps up the Oyu Tolgoi underground copper mine, but expects its annual iron ore shipments from its Australian operations to be little changed in 2025.

Mined copper on a consolidated basis is expected to be in the range of 780kt - 850kt, compared to the 660K - 720K target for 2024.

“As we ramp up the Oyu Tolgoi underground copper mine, deliver the Simandou high-grade iron ore project in Guinea, and build out our lithium business through the proposed acquisition of Arcadium, we are underwriting a decade of profitable growth," Rio Tinto (RIO) Chief Executive Jakob Stausholm said.

The company is targeting annual production of 1 million tonnes of copper by the end of this decade, underpinned by an increase in output from Oyu Tolgoi in Mongolia where production is expected to increase more than 50% next year.

The global miner also projects an increase in overall capital expenditures for fiscal 2025, estimating $11 billion, compared to the $9.5 billion forecasted for 2024.

In other news, Rio Tinto (RIO) partnered with the Swedish investment company Vargas, Mitsubishi Corporation and other international and local industry partners to study a low-carbon aluminium greenfield opportunity in Finland.

As the strategic industrial partner, Rio Tinto will provide the Arctial partnership with access to its AP60 technology and assist in what would be the first AP60 deployment in an aluminium smelter outside Quebec, Canada.

The project, if successful, would be the first primary aluminium development in continental Europe for over 30 years, the company said.

data.title: Crescent Energy prices upsize 21.5M stock offering at $14
data.description:
• Crescent Energy (NYSE:CRGY) has priced its upsized public offering by the issuance of 21.5M shares of Class A common stock (from 18M) at $14/share.
  • Underwriters' overall allotment is an additional 3.23M shares.
  • The company plans to use the net proceeds from its offering to fund a portion of the cash consideration for its acquisition of Ridgemar (Eagle Ford) LLC, expected to close in Q1 2025. The acquisition is not contingent on the completion of the offering, and the proceeds may be used to reduce outstanding borrowings under the revolving credit facility or for general corporate purposes.
  • The closing date is December 5.
  • CRGY is down 5.42% premarket to $14.32 on Wednesday.

data.title: UK's business optimism drops to its lowest since December 2022
data.description:
• Services PMI in the United Kingdom decreased to 50.80 points in November from 52 points in October 2024.
  • Composite PMI in the United Kingdom decreased to 50.50 points in November from 51.80 points in October 2024.
  • The November data indicated a sustained loss of momentum across the service economy, with business activity and new orders expanding at weaker rates than in the previous month.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB).

Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.title: AJA Health and Wellness announces Kevin Klimuk as CFO
data.description:
• AJA Health and Wellness (TSXV:AJA:CA) announces that it has accepted the resignation of Alnesh Mohan as CFO and Eric Tsung as a company director, effective November 29, 2024.
  • The company also announced the appointment of Kevin Klimuk as CFO for the company.
  • Kevin brings over twenty years of expertise in management, accounting, and finance positions.

data.title: European markets tick higher as French no-confidence vote looms
data.description:
London (UKX) -0.2%

Germany (DAX:IND) +0.7%

France (CAC:IND) +0.4%

The Eurozone economy slipped back into contraction in November, while services activity in France declined at the quickest pace since January.

The HCOB Spain Services PMI dropped to 53.1 in November, down from 54.9 in October and slightly below the expected 53.2.

Services PMI in Italy also fell in November.

The pan-European Stoxx 600 (STOXX) edged higher +0.3% on Wednesday as investors braced for an upcoming no-confidence vote in France’s parliament. French lawmakers are expected to vote on the future of Prime Minister Michel Barnier’s minority government this evening. Markets also took note of the latest PMI data from the region, while the autos sector led gains.

The eurozone’s big-three economies of Germany, France and Italy all registered contractions in business activity midway through the final quarter of 2024, data showed.

Elsewhere, South Korean stocks fell as President Yoon Suk Yeol faces impeachment after martial law chaos.

Coming up in the session: UK PMI expected shortly, with data from the U.S. and Canada awaited later in the day.

In the bond market, the U.S. 10-year Treasury yield was up 3 basis points to 4.25%.

Germany's 10-year yield was up 2 basis points at 2.07%.

UK's 10-year yield was up less than 1 basis point to 4.25%.

Currencies: (EUR:USD) (GBP:USD) (CHF:USD)

ETFs: (EWG), (GF), (EWI), (EWQ), (FGM), (DAX), (FLGR), (FXB), (EWU), (FKU), (EWUS), (FLGB), (GREK)

data.title: Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund reports unaudited NAV as of Nov 30, 2024
data.description:
• Kayne Anderson Energy Infrastructure Fund's (NYSE:KYN) unaudited net asset value was $2.5 billion, or $15.03 per share, as of November 30.

  • Its asset coverage ratio with respect to senior securities representing indebtedness was 664%, and its coverage ratio for debt and preferred stock was 503%.
